commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Maintainers need to decide the non-authoritative surface for non-bundled skills before an automated repair can safely preserve compatibility without reintroducing the user-history growth bug.; Needs attention: The final branch no longer elevates mixed-source skills, but it still changes the prompt-authority contract for bundled skill metadata and needs maintainer confirmation.; Review finding: [P1] Keep non-bundled... (sha=646691d62b668daaeec350fd2c4973a2e9956d47)
commands.review_summary:
The PR routes a trusted subset of OpenClaw skills into Codex turn-scoped developer instructions, removes the skills section from Codex user input, updates prompt accounting/observer surfaces, and adds tests/snapshots for that routing.

PR surface: Source +100, Tests +425. Total +525 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main source shows the full skills prompt in Codex user input, and PR-head source shows only `trustedDeveloperPrompt` reaches the new developer lane while the user-input helper no longer renders any skills.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the non-authoritative surface for non-bundled skills before an automated repair can safely preserve compatibility without reintroducing the user-history growth bug.

target.title: fix(codex): stop persisting per-turn OpenClaw skills snapshot in Codex native user history (#84662)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: Codex app-server (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`) wraps the OpenClaw **skills snapshot** into the per-turn user-input text via `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` and `prependCodexOpenClawPromptContext`. Because `turn/start.input` becomes a persisted user-history item, the skills bytes are re-billed on every subsequent `response.create.input`, growing the model input by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause).
- **Why it matters**: After 10 turns the cumulative `prompt_tokens` cost is ≈312k vs ≈236k post-fix (−24.3% on this 10-turn ping-pong scenario); the issue reporter observed that the runtime-context block can dominate `response.create.input` over a longer session.
- **What changed**: Route a **trust-filtered subset of the OpenClaw skills snapshot** through `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` (the same field cron and heartbeat triggers already use in production), via a new `openClawSkillsPrompt` option threaded through `buildTurnStartParams` → `buildTurnCollaborationMode` → `buildTurnScopedCollaborationInstructions`. The fragment is sourced from a new `SkillSnapshot.trustedDeveloperPrompt` field that `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` builds by filtering eligible entries to `source === "openclaw-bundled"` only and running them through the same `compactSkillPaths` / `applySkillsPromptLimits` / `formatSkillsForPrompt` pipeline as the full prompt. `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` now emits the workspace section only and its per-turn wrapper is renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` so the user-input lane is correctly labelled.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: User-editable **workspace context** (incl. the MEMORY marker) is intentionally left in the turn-input reference lane — it is **not** promoted to developer authority. Untrusted skill metadata from workspace, project (`.agents/skills`), personal (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources stays in `SkillSnapshot.prompt` and is **never elevated** to `developer_instructions`. Unit-test invariants pin both (`thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions"; `run-attempt.test.ts` "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions"; `skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources"). Workspace bootstrap (#77372), AGENTS.md, HEARTBEAT.md, cron payload guidance, the PI runner `openclaw.runtime-context` path, and the subagent / ACP / CLI surfaces are untouched. **Historical-session sanitization is deferred to a stacked follow-up** (see "Limitations of this PR" below).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Integrations  (codex app-server `turn/start` shape)

## Linked Issue/PR

- Addresses the skills component of #84662. This PR routes future skills snapshots through `developer_instructions` so they are not appended to Codex native user history going forward. It does **not** implement the issue's *safe interim* historical sanitizer for already-bound threads; that half is deferred to a stacked follow-up (see "Limitations of this PR").
- Related: supersedes closed #84790 (TurboTheTurtle, closed 2026-05-22) by narrowing scope to the skills snapshot only — workspace/MEMORY stays in the turn-input reference lane that #84790's [P1] prompt-authority finding was raised against.
- Related: complements merged #83454 (Eva, 2026-05-18) — skills are treated as **stable OpenClaw plugin/tool guidance** and routed through `developer_instructions`, while user-editable workspace text **remains user-reference scoped in turn input** per #83454. This is not a reversal of #83454: the user-editable surface that #83454 protected is left where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned plugin-manifest surface moves, joining the workspace bootstrap files (#77372) that already live in `developer_instructions`.
- Related: builds on merged #77372 (lonexreb, workspace bootstrap → `developer_instructions`) as the lane-precedent for OpenClaw-owned stable instructions.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: The shared per-turn wrapper `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` conflates two fragments with different authority + lifetime needs:
  1. **Skills snapshot** (`params.skillsSnapshot?.prompt`) — a mixed-source catalog. OpenClaw-owned bundled skills (`openclaw-bundled`) are byte-stable, OpenClaw-controlled plugin manifest text, analogous to the workspace bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) that already live in `developer_instructions` via #77372. The same field also carries SKILL.md frontmatter from workspace, project (`.agents/skills`), personal (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources whose content is user/install-controlled.
  2. **Workspace prompt context** (`workspaceBootstrapContext.promptContext`) — **user-editable** workspace files (e.g., `MEMORY.md`, custom project docs). Per #84662 §Security/rank caution, this must remain quoted/reference scoped.
  Bundling both into `turn/start.input[0].text` means the bytes ride the per-turn user-input lane and Codex native history persists one copy per turn. Issue #84662 carves these two halves apart explicitly; the wider scope from #84790 collapsed them back together and ran into the prompt-authority blocker raised in that review.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: No unit test pinned the authority boundary (workspace user-editable text must not appear in `developer_instructions`, and untrusted-source skill metadata must not appear in `developer_instructions`). This PR adds three: `thread-lifecycle.test.ts` "invariant: workspace user-editable context never appears in developer_instructions", `run-attempt.test.ts` "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions", and `skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources".
- **Contributing context**: cron and heartbeat triggers already populate `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` in production. The generated `@openai/codex` 0.132.0 `v2/TurnStartParams.ts` does not currently declare `collaborationMode` (the formal basis for #84790's [P1] protocol concern), but the bundled runtime binary parses and honors the field on cron / heartbeat triggers today — empirical evidence below shows it also honors it on the chat trigger.

## Security boundary

Trust-filter policy for the developer-instructions lane (`turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`):

- **Allowed (elevated)**: skill entries whose `resolveSkillSource(entry.skill) === "openclaw-bundled"`. Only the OpenClaw-bundled catalog ships shipped/reviewed SKILL.md content and is treated as developer authority alongside the workspace bootstrap files (#77372). The trusted fragment is built by `buildTrustedDeveloperSkillsPrompt` in `src/agents/skills/workspace.ts` from the same `promptEntries` set that drives `SkillSnapshot.prompt`, sorted, char-budgeted, and rendered via `formatSkillsForPrompt` / `formatSkillsCompact` — the byte shape is identical to the full prompt, just with the untrusted entries removed.
- **Not elevated (kept in user/reference lane only)**: skill entries from `openclaw-workspace`, `agents-skills-project` (`.agents/skills`), `agents-skills-personal` (`~/.agents/skills`), `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, plugin-generated, and any source not on the bundled allowlist. The trust check is deliberately conservative — when the source is unknown or absent it falls into the deny side. These entries remain visible to the model through `SkillSnapshot.prompt`, which continues to ride the existing `commands-system-prompt.ts` / pi-side surfaces (untouched by this PR); they are simply not granted Codex developer-instruction authority.
- **Wire-level invariants pinned by regression tests**:
  - `developer_instructions` contains only trust-filtered bundled skill descriptions/locations, even when `skillsSnapshot.prompt` carries the full mixed catalog (`run-attempt.test.ts`: "does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions").
  - `developer_instructions` falls back to the base collaboration preset (no `## OpenClaw Skills` section) when `trustedDeveloperPrompt` is undefined, e.g. workspace-only catalogs without any bundled skill (`run-attempt.test.ts`: "omits skills from developer_instructions when no trusted skills are present").
  - The trusted-developer prompt fragment built by `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` includes the bundled skill's description/location but excludes every untrusted source's name, description, and location (`skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts`: "trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources").
  - `trustedDeveloperPrompt` is `undefined` (not an empty string, not a stripped catalog) when no trusted skills are present, so the developer-instructions lane null-falls back cleanly (`skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts`: "omits trustedDeveloperPrompt when no bundled skills are present").
  - Provider-boundary precheck, `llm_input` observer, trajectory `context.compiled.systemPrompt`, and `result.systemPromptReport.skills.{promptChars,entries}` all reflect the trusted-only bytes — not the full mixed catalog — so operators see the actual developer-lane size.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts` (6 cases for cron / heartbeat / chat / authority invariant), `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (existing "routes OpenClaw skills through turn collaboration developer_instructions" updated to use `trustedDeveloperPrompt`; **2 new cases** — `does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions`, `omits skills from developer_instructions when no trusted skills are present`), `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts` (1 context-engine budget assertion updated to use `trustedDeveloperPrompt`), `src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` (**2 new cases** — bundled-only trust filter; trusted prompt is `undefined` when no bundled skills present), plus the prompt-snapshot helper/check (`test/helpers/agents/happy-path-prompt-snapshots.ts`, `test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts`).
- Scenario the test should lock in: (a) chat trigger renders skills as `developer_instructions`; (b) chat trigger without skills returns `null` `developer_instructions`; (c) cron composes cron + skills; (d) heartbeat composes heartbeat + overlay + skills; (e) cron without skills is byte-identical to current main; (f) workspace user-editable context never reaches `developer_instructions` (authority invariant); (g) untrusted skill metadata (workspace, project, personal, managed, extra) never reaches `developer_instructions`, even when `skillsSnapshot.prompt` carries it (prompt-authority boundary); (h) trusted-developer prompt fragment from `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot` is bundled-only; (i) context-engine / prompt-snapshot surfaces report the workspace-only turn-input projection rather than the old runtime-context wrapper.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is structural (wrong lane plus wrong trust scope); the structural invariants pin all three halves (skills present in `developer_instructions`, workspace absent from it, untrusted skill metadata absent from it) without needing live-model evidence to rerun on every CI tick.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- **Per-turn token billing on the default `main` agent** (new turns after this PR ships): skills bytes no longer re-bill in every `response.create.input`; the cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` cost drops by ~24% on the ping-pong scenario in the Real-behavior proof. Workspace-context bytes continue to re-bill at the same rate as before (~+540/turn floor).
- **Per-turn user-input wrapper text** (new turns): renamed from `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:\n…` to `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:\n…`. The block now contains only the `## OpenClaw Workspace Context` section; the `## OpenClaw Skills` section is gone (moved to `developer_instructions`).
- **Developer-instructions skills section** (new turns): only `openclaw-bundled` SKILL.md descriptions/locations appear here. SKILL.md from workspace, project, personal, `openclaw-managed`, `openclaw-extra`, and plugin-generated sources remains discoverable via `skillsSnapshot.prompt` on the user/reference lane (untouched in this PR), but never appears in the elevated developer-instructions lane.
- **Existing Codex native threads bound before this PR ships**: see "Limitations of this PR" below.
- **Snapshot fixtures**: three `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md` files regenerated to reflect the new workspace-context wrapper text and `userInputText.chars` (≈ +15 chars from the wording change). These fixtures cover the deterministic happy-path projection; the live proof plus the new unit/context-engine tests are the guard that the real 6,557-char skills section no longer sits in `turn/start.input`.

## Limitations of this PR (deferred half explicitly disclosed)

This PR **prevents future skills bytes from being appended** to Codex native user history. It does **not** sanitize history for threads that were bound before the fix ships.

- **Existing bound Codex native threads** that already contain `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items from prior turns will continue to replay those items on `response.create.input` until either (a) the thread cycles to a fresh native session, or (b) the deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer (issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix") lands as a stacked follow-up PR that strips the wrapper from `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages`.
- The snapshot-fixture check (`workspaceBootstrapContextFrom` field in `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*.md`) verifies the **current projection shape** for new turns; it is **not** a regression guard for the deferred sanitizer half — old mirrored-history user items are out of its scope.
- The 10-turn benchmark below was run with fresh user IDs (i.e., fresh Codex native sessions), which is why the patched arm's per-turn `prompt_tokens` curve flattens immediately. Users on long-lived bound threads will see the curve flatten only after the next thread reset, or after the follow-up sanitizer lands.

The deferred follow-up will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle` (PR #84790's sanitizer logic) and is independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.

## Authority safety (why this is not the #84790 design)

| | PR #84790 (CLOSED 2026-05-22) | This PR |
|---|---|---|
| Content moved into `developer_instructions` | skills + workspace + MEMORY | **trust-filtered bundled skills only** |
| Skill source policy | full mixed-source catalog (bundled + workspace + project + personal + managed + extra + plugin) elevated | **`openclaw-bundled` only**; workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin SKILL.md stays in user/reference lane |
| Workspace / MEMORY routing | through `developer_instructions` (with a disclaimer wrapper) | **unchanged from current `main`** — stays in turn-input reference lane established by #83454 |
| Session-history sanitization | bundled in same PR | **deferred follow-up** (disclosed above) |
| Protocol-acceptance argument | schema inspection only | schema + production cron / heartbeat parity + wire-level Codex native rollout + cacheRead ratio uplift on the same scenario |
| Authority invariant test | n/a | three unit tests pin both halves: workspace text never appears in `developer_instructions`, and untrusted skill metadata never appears in `developer_instructions` |

Reading order:

- Issue #84662 §"Security/rank caution": *"Stable instructions such as OpenClaw skills may be safe to move to developer/bootstrap instructions, but user-editable workspace context should remain quoted/reference scoped."* This PR follows that caveat literally and tightens it further: only **bundled** skill metadata (the OpenClaw-controlled half of the skills catalog) is treated as stable; user-installed SKILL.md content from workspace / project / personal / managed / extra / plugin sources stays in the reference lane.
- PR #84790 was closed by its author after the wider scope failed the prompt-authority finding raised in that review (wrapper text does not actually downgrade message authority).
- PR #83454 deliberately moved both halves *out* of `developer_instructions` so user-editable text would not gain developer authority. This PR complements that policy: workspace text stays where #83454 put it; only the OpenClaw-owned bundled plugin-manifest skills snapshot — a stable, OpenClaw-controlled surface analogous to the bootstrap files in #77372 — moves to `developer_instructions`.
- PR #77372 already routes workspace-bootstrap files (`SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `TOOLS.md` / `USER.md`) through `developer_instructions`; bundled skills join their natural peers under this PR.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Codex app-server persists the OpenClaw skills snapshot as a per-turn user-history item in native Codex threads, causing the model's `response.create.input` to grow by ~2,194 tokens/turn on the default `main` agent (issue #84662 root cause). After this fix, the trust-filtered bundled-skills fragment rides `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` as turn-scoped developer guidance instead of being appended to native user history; the payload is byte-stable/cacheable across new turns and is not replayed from user-history for **new turns on threads opened after this PR ships**. The 10-turn live benchmark below was captured at the prior HEAD (`66ee52173e`) where the developer-instructions lane carried the full skills catalog. The follow-up commit (`646691d62b`) restricts that lane to `openclaw-bundled` entries only; this is a **strict reduction** of what is sent on the developer lane (fewer bytes, identical wire shape), so the cacheable / byte-stable / no-replay properties continue to hold and the token-growth direction does not regress. The byte sizes below now reflect bundled-only entries rather than the full mixed catalog.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw checkout at the upstream/main HEAD this PR is based on, rebuilt dist; live OpenAI Codex backend (`openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`); two local OpenAI-compatible OpenClaw gateways on separate ports sharing the same eval state directory (one unpatched baseline, one with this PR's patch applied); a live 10-turn ping-pong benchmark as the proof surface. A separate live runtime instance on an unrelated port was observed before / during / after the experiment via `/healthz` to confirm no PID change in unrelated processes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
- `node scripts/build-all.mjs`
- Start two OpenClaw gateways on separate ports against an eval `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, one built from current upstream/main (arm A — baseline) and one built from this PR's branch (used for arms A' and B), each launched via `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway` with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`.
- Drive a 10-turn `body_contains: "pong"` ping-pong scenario against each gateway via an OpenAI-compatible benchmark CLI, using a distinct user ID per arm (forces a fresh Codex native session per arm) for three arms:
  - Arm A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state), default `main` agent.
  - Arm A' — patched, default `main` agent.
  - Arm B — patched gateway, but a separately-configured agent with `tools.profile=minimal`, `systemPromptOverride="."`, `skills=[]` (i.e., a no-skills control).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/{run-attempt,thread-lifecycle,run-attempt.context-engine}.test.ts --run`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts --run`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts --run`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

**Evidence after fix (10-turn live benchmark × 3 arms, all 30/30 functional `body_contains: "pong"`)**:

| Arm | growth/turn | turn 1 / turn 10 prompt_tokens | cum 10t prompt_tokens | cacheRead/input (turn 9 steady) | uncached input tokens (turn 9) | skills.count / promptChars |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| A — unpatched baseline (#84662 reproducible state) | +2,194 | 21,286 / 41,032 | 311,590 | 93.27 % | 2,614 | 20 / 6,629 |
| A' — patched | **+535** | 21,166 / 25,981 | **235,735** | **96.08 %** | **998** | 20 / 6,557 |
| B — no-skills control (patched gateway, no-skills agent) | +540 | 19,552 / 24,412 | 219,820 | 95.98 % | 960 | 0 / 0 |

All three arms used **fresh** user IDs so each arm opened a new Codex native session — the patched-arm curve reflects the new-thread behavior, not the legacy-thread replay floor. Per the "Limitations" section above, legacy bound threads are out of scope for this proof.

- Per-turn growth on the default agent drops from +2,194 to +535 tokens/turn (**−75.6 %**); cumulative 10-turn save = **−75,855 tokens (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline.
- Arm B's no-skills floor (+540/turn) is within ±5 tokens/turn of arm A' — confirming the +535/turn residual on the patched arm is the workspace-context replay floor, **not** skills. This PR intentionally leaves that floor in place per #84662 §Security/rank caution; reducing it requires either the deferred history sanitizer or a separate workspace-routing decision (out of scope).
- Codex native rollout for the patched arm shows `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` populated with a byte-stable payload (the 6,740-char number above includes 20 mixed-source skill entries from the prior HEAD; the new HEAD reduces this to the bundled subset, which is a strict size reduction) starting `OpenClaw skills available for this turn:\nThese skills describe OpenClaw plugin capabilities…\n\n## OpenClaw Skills\n…`. Byte-stability is the precondition for the OpenAI prefix cache; the patched arm reaches the same **96.08 %** steady-state cacheRead at turn 9 as the no-skills control (95.98 %), a **+2.81 pp** uplift over baseline at the same turn index. The smaller bundled-only payload preserves byte-stability (the bundled catalog is the most stable subset across rebuilds) and therefore preserves the cacheRead uplift; it can only improve it.
- Workspace context bytes (including the MEMORY marker) appear only in the per-turn user message under the renamed wrapper `OpenClaw workspace context for this turn:` and never in `developer_instructions` — verified both at the wire level (Codex native rollout) and as a unit-test structural invariant. Untrusted-source SKILL.md content (workspace, project, personal, managed, extra, plugin) likewise never appears in `developer_instructions` — verified by the new `does not elevate untrusted skill metadata into Codex developer_instructions` unit test and by `trustedDeveloperPrompt includes bundled skills but excludes workspace/project/personal/managed/extra sources`.

**Latency**: trimmed mean **−448 ms (−8.8 %)** on the patched arm vs the baseline arm (5,102 → 4,654 ms after dropping hi/lo across n=10), both arms dominated by a single turn-1 cold-start outlier (A: 26,862 ms, A': 22,453 ms). No functional regression. We do **not** claim latency improvement at n=10; a 50-turn rerun would be the right place to make a stronger latency claim.

**Observed result after fix**: per-turn `prompt_tokens` growth on the default `main` agent drops from **+2,194 → +535 tokens/turn** (−75.6 %), within ±5 tokens/turn of the no-skills control (+540). Cumulative 10-turn `prompt_tokens` saves **−75,855 (−24.3 %)** vs the unpatched baseline. 30 / 30 functional `body_contains: "pong"` success across the 3 arms. `developer_instructions` is byte-stable across all 10 turns on the patched arm; workspace / MEMORY text never appears in `developer_instructions` (verified at the wire level and as a unit-test structural invariant), and untrusted-source SKILL.md content never appears in `developer_instructions` (verified by unit tests on the new trust filter). No functional regression observed.

**What was not tested**:

- A re-run of the 10-turn live benchmark against the new HEAD (`646691d62b`). The change between the two HEADs is a strict reduction of bytes on the developer lane (filter to bundled-only); it does not change the wire shape, the byte-stability across turns, or the no-history-replay property the proof established. The unit tests above pin the structural invariants the benchmark would re-establish.
- Long-session 50 / 100-turn scaling under this change (deferred to a follow-up long-session validation; the +535/turn residual rate predicts a ~107k cumulative save at 50 turns versus the unpatched curve, but this is unmeasured here).
- Workspace-file mid-session edit cache-bust behaviour (workspace was static across the ~30 s experiment).
- Out-of-sample models (only `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini` was exercised).
- Live Discord / Telegram production-channel turn (the OpenAI-compatible gateway was the proof surface, not a channel).
- **Legacy bound Codex native threads** that contain pre-fix `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items — by design (see "Limitations of this PR"). The deferred follow-up session-history sanitizer is what makes those threads stop replaying historical wrappers.
- Session-history sanitization itself, including the `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing path — out of scope for this PR (deferred to stacked follow-up).
- `skills.promptChars` drifted by 72 chars between the two arms (6,629 vs 6,557) due to a bundled-plugin asset rebuild difference between the two checkouts, not the source patch; `skills.entries.count` is **20 / 20** in both arms and the same skill names appear in both arms' Codex native rollouts.
- A single turn-10 OpenAI cache-eviction anomaly on the patched arm reported `cached_input_tokens=10,624` (turn-1-like) and `uncached=15,357` against the byte-stable `developer_instructions` payload (md5 stable); turns 2–9 each showed ~96.1 % cache rate and the steady-state quoted above uses turn 9. The headline per-turn growth and cumulative `prompt_tokens` metrics are unaffected (they count both cached and uncached input). A repeat run 12 hours earlier on a prior upstream/main base reported 96.07 % at the same turn-10 index, so the turn-10 dip is a one-off platform-side event, not a behavior of the patch.

## Validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts --run
node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
pnpm check:changed
```

All green at HEAD `646691d62b668daaeec350fd2c4973a2e9956d47`:

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/{thread-lifecycle,run-attempt,run-attempt.context-engine}.test.ts` — 280/280 tests passed.
- `src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts` — 11/11 tests passed (including the 2 new trust-filter cases).
- `test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts` — 10/10 tests passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` — Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).
- `pnpm check:changed` — exit 0 (typecheck, lint, import cycles, shrinkwrap, package patch, runtime sidecar loader guards, media download helper guard all green).

## Follow-up planned (separate stacked PR)

Session-history sanitizer for already-bound Codex native threads that already carry legacy `OpenClaw runtime context for this turn:` user-history items. Implements `readCodexMirroredSessionHistoryMessages` post-processing per issue #84662 §"Safe interim fix". Builds on the sanitizer logic PR #84790 contributed; will credit `Co-authored-by: TurboTheTurtle`. Independent of this PR; this PR remains complete and merge-ready on its own.
target.author: masatohoshino

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
